item.title: Sales headcount shift (2026-04-17)
item.description:
Something quietly shifted in how companies think about sales headcount.
Three stats from the last 90 days changed how I look at this:
Block laid off 4,000 people. Stock jumped 18% the same day. Salesforce cut support from 9,000 to 5,000 in under a year. Meta now generates $2.4M revenue per employee. Up 85% over 3 years.
The common thread isn't "AI replaced the team." It's "AI replaced the repetitive 70%, and humans kept the closing conversations."
36% of B2B companies cut their SDR team in 2025. 22% already replaced SDRs with AI agents. 87% of sales orgs use AI. Only 24% use the agentic kind.
That last gap is where the next 18 months play out.
What are you seeing on your side?
